title: antidesma rufescens
intro: Antidesma rufescens, also known as the red-berried coral tree or red-berried wood's hardy, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Antidesma rufescens include its potential to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. The plant's leaves and fruit can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are commonly consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
